The '''hitcher crab''' was a [[Crustacean/Legends|crustacean]] native to the [[Desert/Legends|deserts]] of [[Sevarcos II]]. They moved around the desert, and ate [[Plant/Legends|plants]] and smaller animals. A slow-acting [[Poison/Legends|poison]] coated their claws and shell, used both to disable prey and to discourage predators. With medical treatment, the poison was generally non-fatal for [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. The [[Sevari]] often hunted them, both for their nutritious meat, and for their large [[Water/Legends|water]] sacs.<ref name="CG" />

During summer months, the crabs would bury themselves in the sand to avoid the intense heat. This made the spiky shells a hidden hazard for unwary animals and traveling beings. However, the hitcher crabs also had substantially reduced body functions, and were more vulnerable to hunters.<ref name="CG" />

During the mating season, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] and female crab would [[Reproduction/Legends|mate]] and form a pack. After three weeks of gestation, the female would lay four to eight [[Egg/Legends|eggs]], which would hatch in nine months. After that, the female newborns would depart and be raised with the mother, while the father raised the males.<ref name="CG" />

Hitcher crabs became full-grown adults at 15 [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]].<ref name="CG" />
